rows.agency: Department of War Office of Strategic Capital
rows.program: critical minerals and rare-earth processing
rows.title: Energy Fuels
rows.award_text: $725M conditional loan commitment for U.S. rare-earth separation and metallization capacity
rows.investing_angle: Most direct public-company funding catalyst in the attached scans; material policy-backed financing for UUUU's rare-earth pivot, but still not closed.
rows.next_trigger: Final loan close terms, project capex budget, customer/offtake evidence, and same-session price/flow confirmation.

rows.agency: Department of War / Air Force / Space Systems Command
rows.program: Mobile User Objective System service life extension Phase II
rows.title: Boeing
rows.award_text: $2.003B fixed-price-incentive contract to design, build, launch-support, and test two MUOS satellites; $204.1M obligated at award
rows.investing_angle: Large direct Boeing defense-space win; review whether satellite backlog offsets commercial-aircraft noise enough to affect BA watch/research posture.
rows.next_trigger: Task milestones, obligation ramp, program-margin commentary, follow-on satellite demand, and same-session price/flow confirmation.

rows.program: Precision Strike Missiles Increment One production capacity and EOC procurement
rows.title: Lockheed Martin
rows.award_text: $8.402B modification to increase the ceiling and extend ordering for PrSM Increment One production capacity, EOC assets, follow-on production, development, and obsolescence management; total contract face value now $13.34B
rows.investing_angle: Major direct missile-production backlog signal; review whether PrSM demand/replenishment changes LMT defense growth priority versus already-priced defense strength.
rows.next_trigger: Follow-on funded orders, production-rate disclosure, margin/backlog commentary, stock reaction, and whether PrSM replenishment demand persists.

rows.agency: Department of War / Missile Defense Agency
rows.program: Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) Interceptor multi-year procurement
rows.recipient: Lockheed Martin Missiles and Fire Control
rows.title: Lockheed Martin Missiles and Fire Control
rows.award_text: $35.327B sole-source fixed-price-incentive undefinitized contract for THAAD interceptor production; $842.9M obligated at award; performance March 2026-June 2032
rows.investing_angle: Largest same-day direct public-company funding item; materially reinforces LMT missile-defense backlog but still needs margin, obligation cadence, and already-priced-defense-strength checks before posture changes.
rows.next_trigger: Obligation ramp, interceptor production-rate disclosure, margin/backlog commentary, missile-defense budget follow-through, and same-session price/flow confirmation.

rows.program: GPS IIIF Space Vehicles 23 and 24
rows.title: Lockheed Martin
rows.award_text: $514M contract for two GPS IIIF satellites
rows.investing_angle: National-security space backlog support and resilient PNT exposure, but slower-moving than a near-term trade trigger.
risks: backlog support rather than immediate earnings catalyst
rows.next_trigger: Space Force follow-on awards, margin detail, or national-security space budget acceleration.

rows.program: CHIPS indium phosphide production expansion
rows.title: Coherent
rows.award_text: Up to $50M letter of intent to expand Coherent's Sherman, Texas indium phosphide wafer facility
rows.investing_angle: Clean public CHIPS-linked photonics beneficiary tied to AI datacenter optical interconnect supply chain.
rows.next_trigger: Final CHIPS award terms, capex/production milestones, datacenter-photonics customer demand evidence.

rows.agency: Department of War / Defense Innovation Unit
rows.program: Drone Dominance Program
rows.title: Drone Dominance Program vendor pool
rows.award_text: $1.1B two-year drone program; first batch accepted, 30,000 drones ordered/delivering, 60,000 more planned for September; no public-company recipients named in the official update
rows.investing_angle: Material defense-autonomy procurement backdrop, but no named public beneficiary; keep as theme watch only until awardees or task-order economics are disclosed.
risks: future orders conditional on competitive events
unit economics and supplier mix undisclosed
rows.next_trigger: Named vendor awards, public-company recipient disclosure, task-order dollars, production economics, or public-company exposure confirmation.

rows.program: Collaborative Combat Aircraft mission autonomy production options
rows.recipient: RTX Collins Aerospace / Anduril / Shield AI; pool includes Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman
rows.title: RTX Collins Aerospace / Anduril / Shield AI; pool includes Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman
rows.award_text: CCA mission-autonomy production options awarded; dollar values not disclosed
rows.investing_angle: Strategic autonomy-software tailwind; RTX has the cleanest public production-option read-through but dollars are undisclosed.
rows.next_trigger: Dollarized task orders, primary autonomy-provider selection, software license economics, or summer 2027 CCA follow-on detail.

rows.program: CHIPS R&D AI-driven semiconductor materials
rows.title: SandboxAQ
rows.award_text: $500M CHIPS R&D award plus Commerce minority non-voting equity stake
rows.investing_angle: Useful semis/materials supply-chain signal, but direct recipient is private; public tickers are read-throughs only.
rows.next_trigger: Public partner/customer detail, tool-vendor demand evidence, or company-specific revenue linkage.

rows.program: P-8A training systems support IDIQ
rows.title: Boeing
rows.award_text: $880M ceiling IDIQ for P-8A training systems support, training materials, technical publications, and installation services through June 2033; no funds obligated at award
rows.investing_angle: Large defense-services backlog support for Boeing, but the IDIQ ceiling and zero initial obligation make it watch context rather than a same-day capital decision.
rows.next_trigger: Funded task orders, margin commentary, or broader P-8 sustainment budget acceleration.

rows.program: Naval ship maintenance and modernization IDIQ
rows.title: HII Fleet Support Group
rows.award_text: $417.7M ceiling IDIQ for maintenance, modernization, and repair for non-nuclear Navy surface ships; no funds obligated at award
rows.investing_angle: Direct public defense backlog support for HII's fleet-services lane, but task-order conversion is the key evidence before it affects posture.
rows.next_trigger: Funded task orders, Navy surface-ship maintenance tempo, or company backlog/margin commentary.

rows.program: NAVFAC electrical engineering and design IDIQ
rows.title: Jacobs Government Services
rows.award_text: $249M maximum-value IDIQ for electrical engineering, design, studies, and related services in Hawaii and other NAVFAC Pacific locations; $5K minimum guarantee obligated
rows.investing_angle: Jacobs gets a sizable federal engineering ceiling tied to Pacific infrastructure, but the minimum guarantee is tiny and revenue depends on future task orders.
rows.next_trigger: Material task orders, Pacific defense infrastructure funding pull-through, or company commentary on federal services growth.

rows.agency: Department of Homeland Security / Customs and Border Protection
rows.program: Medium Energy Mobile Systems IDIQ
rows.title: Leidos
rows.award_text: Potential $270M IDIQ to deliver up to 100 mobile non-intrusive inspection systems over five years
rows.investing_angle: Direct backlog support for border-security inspection systems, but IDIQ/task-order timing keeps it watch context.
risks: secondary source until official award detail is fetched
rows.next_trigger: Official CBP award notice, task orders, margin detail, or follow-on deployment evidence.

rows.program: Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition Program On-Ramp 2
rows.title: NASA CSDA On-Ramp 2 public awardees
rows.award_text: NASA CSDA On-Ramp 2 added public/private contract holders under a $476M shared IDIQ; no individual task-order dollars disclosed
rows.investing_angle: Same-day official space-data procurement signal supports commercial Earth-observation demand, with Planet Labs and Airbus as public read-throughs, but no individual task-order dollars were disclosed.
rows.next_trigger: Company-specific NASA task orders, CSDA usage growth, revenue/margin disclosure, or follow-on federal remote-sensing demand.

rows.agency: Department of War / Defense Innovation Unit
rows.program: Quantum sensing and timing ISR initiative
rows.title: DIU quantum sensing solution-brief participants
rows.award_text: DIU initiative expected to invest up to $200M within the next year in quantum electric-field sensors, magnetometers, gravimeters, tactical clocks, and component insertions; no public-company awards named yet
rows.investing_angle: A material defense quantum demand signal, but no named public-company recipient; watch for direct awards to quantum, defense electronics, timing, or sensor suppliers before changing posture.
rows.next_trigger: Named DIU awards, public-company recipient disclosure, task-order dollars, production economics, or partner/customer linkage.

rows.program: Family of Maintenance Trainers IDIQ
rows.title: TRU Simulation + Training
rows.award_text: $396M firm-fixed-price IDIQ for Army family of maintenance trainers, including analysis, design, development, production, installation, integration, cybersecurity, and instruction
rows.investing_angle: Meaningful Textron training-systems backlog support; review whether defense training/simulation demand changes TXT research priority, but keep below LMT/BA due IDIQ/task-order uncertainty.
risks: smaller relative to Textron consolidated revenue
rows.next_trigger: Task-order dollars, revenue timing, margin commentary, and whether trainer demand appears in broader Textron defense backlog.

rows.program: National Quantum Virtual Laboratory design competition
rows.recipient: Five university-led NQVL design teams with public/private partners
rows.title: Five university-led NQVL design teams with public/private partners
rows.award_text: $20M total NSF investment; five teams receive $4M over two years; public-company partners include Boeing, Honeywell, IonQ, NVIDIA, Quantinuum and others
rows.investing_angle: Useful quantum-sensing/network/computing ecosystem signal, but public companies are partners/read-throughs rather than direct awardees; keep as watch context for defense/AI/quantum themes.
risks: public companies are partners not awardees
rows.next_trigger: Named company tasking, implementation-phase awards, disclosed commercial contracts, or direct revenue linkage to public partners.

rows.program: Advanced Medium Range Air-to-Air Missile D4/C9 preliminary design and proof-of-manufacturing
rows.title: Raytheon
rows.award_text: $398.7M cost-plus-fixed-fee and cost-reimbursement undefinitized contract action for AMRAAM system requirements/preliminary design review activities; $104.1M FMS funds obligated; awarded June 23
rows.investing_angle: Direct RTX missile backlog and allied-air-defense demand support, but early design scope and cost-type structure make it research context below LMT THAAD.
risks: design-review scope rather than full-rate production
rows.next_trigger: Follow-on production award, booked backlog commentary, margin detail, allied demand acceleration, and tape/flow confirmation.

rows.agency: Department of War / Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office
rows.program: Agent Network AI-enabled battle management and targeting Pace-Setting Project
rows.recipient: Department of War Agent Network participants
rows.title: Department of War Agent Network participants
rows.award_text: Department of War launched Agent Network, an AI-enabled battle-management and targeting capability; release says the second Pace-Setting Project builds on foundational Command and Control innovation by Palantir Technologies and Lumbra, but disclosed no new dollar award
rows.investing_angle: Official defense-AI demand signal supports PLTR defense-software watch context, but no contract value or new award was disclosed, so this is not a research_review or add-path catalyst.
risks: program launch rather than contract notice
Palantir named as foundation not sole recipient
valuation sensitivity in defense AI names
rows.next_trigger: Named award or task order, program dollar disclosure, Maven/Agent Network revenue commentary, or verified market/tape reaction tied to the official release.

rows.agency: Department of Commerce / CHIPS Research & Development Office
rows.program: CHIPS R&D silicon-carbide semiconductor award
rows.title: I-Pulse
rows.award_text: $250M definitive CHIPS R&D award to develop next-generation silicon-carbide semiconductors for harsh-environment high-temperature, high-current, high-voltage switching; Commerce received a minority, non-controlling equity stake
rows.investing_angle: Private direct recipient; useful watch context for U.S. SiC, pulsed-power drilling, geothermal, fusion, advanced manufacturing, and critical-minerals productivity, but no clean public-company beneficiary or immediate capital decision.
risks: government equity stake may complicate future financing
rows.next_trigger: Named public manufacturing partner, follow-on production award, disclosed SiC supply-chain/customer economics, or public-company revenue linkage.

rows.program: Integrated conventional stores management system repair services
rows.title: Boeing
rows.award_text: $17.93M sole-source requirements contract for line-replaceable-unit repair services
rows.investing_angle: Useful sustainment work but immaterial for Boeing.
rows.next_trigger: Ignore unless tied to a broader sustainment or margin trend.

rows.program: MK 54 lightweight torpedo sonar assembly kits
rows.title: General Dynamics Mission Systems
rows.award_text: $116.6M modification for MK 54 MOD 1 lightweight torpedo sonar assembly kits, test equipment, spares, and support
rows.investing_angle: Real undersea-warfare backlog increment, but small relative to GD scale.
rows.next_trigger: Larger follow-on undersea awards or margin/backlog detail.

rows.program: Global Nurse Advice Line bridge support
rows.title: OptumHealth Care Solutions
rows.award_text: $25.3M bridge modification for Military Health System nurse-advice-line support
rows.investing_angle: Real revenue but immaterial for UnitedHealth; maintenance only.
rows.next_trigger: Only relevant if larger Military Health System outsourcing awards follow.

rows.program: M1A2 System Enhancement Package tank production support
rows.title: General Dynamics Land Systems
rows.award_text: $43.5M cost-plus-fixed-fee modification for support of M1A2 System Enhancement Package tank production
rows.investing_angle: Real direct defense work but too small relative to General Dynamics to affect Investing OS posture unless part of a broader armor-production acceleration.
risks: program support rather than thesis-changing award
rows.next_trigger: Ignore unless linked to larger tank-production funding, margin guidance, or multi-year armor demand acceleration.

rows.program: on-orbit refueling and fuel-depot demonstration
rows.title: Quantum Space
rows.award_text: Contract to advance on-orbit refueling/fuel-depot capabilities; value not disclosed
rows.investing_angle: Space-logistics watch item only; public exposure depends on proposed SPAC transaction closing.
rows.next_trigger: Official contract details, SPAC closing, value disclosure, or DoW follow-on funding.

rows.agency: Department of War / Army Corps of Engineers ERDC
rows.program: High-performance computing system production use, delivery, installation, and maintenance
rows.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise
rows.award_text: $28M firm-fixed-price contract; cumulative face value $43.6M; work/funding by order through March 2035
rows.investing_angle: Direct federal HPC win maps to AI/HPC infrastructure themes, but size is too small for a capital decision unless it becomes part of a broader federal supercomputing cycle.
rows.next_trigger: Larger ERDC/HPC follow-on awards, material Cray/supercomputing backlog disclosure, or broader federal AI/HPC procurement acceleration.

rows.program: USS John C. Stennis refueling complex overhaul supplemental work and schedule incentive
rows.title: Huntington Ingalls Newport News Shipbuilding
rows.award_text: $44.1M cost-plus-incentive-fee modification for CVN 74 RCOH supplemental work; $15M obligated at award
rows.investing_angle: Real HII shipbuilding backlog support, but the modification is small versus HII scale and should stay watch-only unless carrier/shipyard margin evidence changes.
rows.next_trigger: Larger Navy shipbuilding awards, carrier RCOH margin/schedule commentary, or backlog acceleration evidence.

summary.top_signal: Energy Fuels: $725M conditional loan commitment for U.S. rare-earth separation and metallization capacity
